title: Show follower lists and counts publicly to all users

requirements.summary: Display follower count as a clickable element in UserProfileView that opens a modal listing all followers with display names, accessible to all visitors including unauthenticated users
acceptanceCriteria: The follower count is visible on every user's profile page for all visitors (logged in or not).
Clicking the follower count opens a list showing each follower's display name.
The followers list is fetched using the public backend query from REQ-1.
The list is accessible on both the current user's own profile and other users' profiles.

file_operations.description: Add a new useGetFollowersList hook that calls backend.getFollowers(user) without authentication requirements. This hook accepts a Principal parameter and returns an array of follower Principals. Since getFollowers is already a public query in the backend interface, this hook can be invoked by any visitor (authenticated or anonymous). Use enabled: !!user to conditionally enable the query only when a valid user principal is provided.

Create a new modal component that displays a list of followers for a given user. Accept a user Principal prop and an onClose callback. Use the useGetFollowersList hook to fetch the array of follower Principals. For each follower Principal, call useGetUserProfile to retrieve their display name (or fall back to a truncated principal string if no profile exists). Render a scrollable list showing each follower's avatar (if available), display name, and a link to their profile. Include a close button in the modal header. Use the Dialog component from frontend/src/components/ui for consistent styling.

Modify the follower count display in UserProfileView to be clickable. Wrap the follower count text in a button element with cursor-pointer styling and an onClick handler that opens the FollowersModal. Maintain a local state boolean (e.g., isFollowersModalOpen) to control modal visibility. Pass the user's Principal to the FollowersModal when rendering it. Ensure the follower count remains visible and clickable for all visitors, including anonymous users. The existing follower count is already fetched via useGetCounts; no changes to that logic are needed.
